key_insight: H-Res adapts large Transformer memories by steering activations on the manifold, changing the effective energy landscape without altering frozen weights or adding prompt tokens.
engineering_relevance: This offers a lower-overhead adaptation route for associative retrieval systems, preserving stability while improving task-specific performance in structured domains.
method: Learns a state-dependent vector field that guides token trajectories toward task-specific attractors.
Formulates adaptation as a control problem on the activation manifold rather than direct synaptic updates.
Keeps the foundation model’s sequence length and global equilibrium unchanged.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports 26% better performance than global weight modification on associative retrieval tasks and claims scalability to structured-domain benchmarks.
watch_item: Manifold steering may require careful training and control design to ensure the learned vector field remains stable and does not distort retrieval behavior under distribution shift.
paper_title: Parallel Manifold Steering: Efficient Adaptation of Large Associative Memories via Residual Energy Shaping
